summary: A stellar sound‑board capture of the legendary Dec 6 1973 Cleveland show, highlighted by a soaring 43‑minute Dark Star and blazing Here Comes Sunshine.
review: The recording is an excellent SBD source – clear, punchy and surprisingly clean for a 1973 tape, with only minor patch or splice quirks. Musically the night shines thanks to a monumental Dark Star, a fiery China Cat Sunflower > Rider transition and one of the most impassioned Here Comes Sunshine performances of the era; the rest of the set flows well despite occasional first‑set sluggishness.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 60 reviews, most rating 5 stars and praising both performance (especially Dark Star, Here Comes Sunshine, China>Rider) and sound quality; only a handful of low or mixed scores with specific complaints about patches/splices, giving a clear consensus of an outstanding show with a very good recording.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crystal‑clear mix for its era, strong bass and piano presence; occasional audience buzz from the AUD patch at the end of Sunshine and a few splice/patch glitches are noted but do not detract from overall listenability.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > The Other One / Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is solid with a memorable China>Rider and strong Sunshine, though some find it a bit sluggish; the second set erupts into an epic Dark Star that dominates the night, followed by a refreshing Eyes and closing Sunshine, delivering a well‑balanced arc.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for inspired playing, powerful guitar tone and occasional distortion bites; described as ‘belting out lyrics’ and ‘truly inspired.’
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for energetic lead‑bass work, massive bomb bursts, and driving the latter half of Dark Star with “super‑energetic” intensity.
band_member_comments.Bob: Few mentions – some note his guitar recedes in the long Dark Star while his vocals are clear when they appear.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s Fender Rhodes/organ texture is lauded as a key spacey element that shapes the Dark Star and other jams; many reviewers call it “key to this Dark Star.”
band_member_comments.Drums: No specific commentary beyond a solid, unobtrusive foundation; drums are considered competent with no notable issues.
